description: How to raise a concern or complain about the service you get from the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) or from an organisation that provides its services.

details.body:
When to raise a concern or complaint

You can raise a concern or make a complaint if you’re unhappy with the service provided by DWP, for example if: 

  • mistakes have been made 
  • there were unreasonable delays 
  • you feel you’ve been treated poorly 
  • you haven’t been kept informed

Read our service standards in our customer charter.

We will not investigate complaints: 

  • about government policy or law 
  • that have already been investigated or are currently being investigated by the Independent Case Examiner or the Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman 
  • about an organisation that provides DWP services, for example employment programmes or health assessment providers - please contact them directly
  • that are, or have been, subject to legal proceedings, including legal settlements

Challenge or appeal a benefit decision 

If you want to challenge or appeal a benefit decision, overpayment decision, child maintenance assessment or sanction you will need to request a mandatory reconsideration.

If you need someone to act on your behalf 

You can ask someone else to raise a concern or make a complaint for you. You’ll need to authorise them to deal with DWP on your behalf first.

Raise a concern directly

If you have a concern about any aspect of the service you have received, let the team or business area you have been dealing with know as soon as possible.

You need to let them know:

  • your National Insurance number – unless you are an employer or landlord
  • your full name, address and contact numbers 
  • which benefit or business area you are raising a concern about 
  • what happened, when it happened and how it affected you 
  • what you want them to do to put things right 

If you’ve experienced unfair treatment, we may consider:

  • making an apology
  • providing an explanation
  • taking corrective action
  • making a special payment if you suffered financially

Who to contact

You can contact the team or business area you’ve been dealing with by phone, in person or in writing. Use the contact details at the top of any recent letters we’ve sent you or the contact details in the list below.

If you live in Northern Ireland, go to the Department for Communities website for more information.

Find out about call charges.

Universal Credit

Raise a concern about Universal Credit online through:

  • the ‘Make a complaint’ service
  • the journal on your Universal Credit account

Universal Credit helpline

Telephone: 0800 328 5644
Relay UK (if you cannot hear or speak on the phone): 18001 then 0800 328 5644

British Sign Language (BSL) video relay service

Monday to Friday, 8am to 8pm

The video relay service uses a free plugin. You may need to change the security settings on your device to install this.

Check if you can use the video relay service

The video relay service uses a free plugin. You may need to change the security settings on your device to install this.

Check if you can use the video relay service on a mobile or tablet

Ask for your concern to go to the complaints team

You can ask for your concern to be reviewed by the DWP complaints team if: 

  • you’re unhappy with the response from the business area you’ve been dealing with initially 
  • your concern has not been fully resolved 

A member of our complaints team will contact you to discuss your complaint in more detail. They will investigate the complaint and aim to resolve it within 15 working days. If the investigation will take longer than 15 days, the complaints resolution manager will keep you updated and tell you when you can expect a response.   

You will receive a final response by telephone or letter from the DWP complaints team. This will tell you the outcome of your complaint. If you need the final response in an alternative format, let us know during your initial call with the complaints team. We’ll inform you if your chosen format will result in any delay.

If you’ve experienced unfair treatment, we may consider:

  • making an apology
  • providing an explanation
  • taking corrective action
  • making a special payment if you suffered financially

You will also be told how to contact the Independent Case Examiner (ICE) if you are still dissatisfied.

Contact the Independent Case Examiner (ICE)

You must have received a final response from the DWP complaints team before you can contact the Independent Case Examiner.

If you’re not happy with the final response from the DWP complaints team, you can ask the Independent Case Examiner to look at your complaint.  

You must contact them within 6 months of receiving the final response from DWP. The details of how to do this will be explained in your response from the complaints team. The Independent Case Examiner may ask to see a copy of your final response, if you received it by letter.  

Find out how to contact the Independent Case Examiner.

Contact the Parliamentary Health Service Ombudsman (PHSO) 

If you’re not happy with the final response from the Independent Case Examiner, you can ask an MP to refer your complaint to the Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman. 

You must contact them within 12 months of receiving the final response from the ICE. The details of how to do this will be explained in your response from the Independent Case Examiner. The Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman may ask to see a copy of your final response, if you received it by letter.  

Find out how to contact the Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman.

Complaints about the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) 

If you need to write to us about a complaint about DWP which is not related to the service you have received, write to: 

DWP Complaints 
Post Handling Site B 
Wolverhampton 
WV99 2GY

change_history.note: DWP's complaints procedure has been updated to reflect the current process for raising a concern or making a complaint.
Removed the textphone number.
Information has been added about the Government response to Parliamentary and Health Service Ombudsman’s Investigation into Women’s State Pension age communications and associated issues.
Removed information about the handling of complaints during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Added translation
Updated opening hours for English language telephony lines for Maternity Allowance and Social Fund.
Updated 'State Pension age changes' to remove instructions for customers seeking redress following the publication of the Ombudsman's report.
Closing time for State Pension and the Pension Service telephone line updated to 6pm.
Updated opening times for State Pension and the Pension Service phone lines and BSL video relay service.
Hours of the Social Fund phone line have reverted back to 8am to 5pm
Added translation
The opening hours of the Social Fund Enquiry Line have changed to Monday to Friday, 10am to 3pm.
Updating Welsh page to match English.
Updated contact information to contact the Child Maintenance Service.
Vaccine damage payment complaints contact information has been removed, as this is no longer managed by DWP.
DWP definition of a complaint added.

The complaints procedure itself has not changed, but the steps involved in making a complaint have been made clearer.
Welsh page updated to reflect changes to English.
Updated telephone numbers for 'Death and Bereavement benefits complaints'.
Updated the links to the video relay service for British Sign Language (BSL) users.

The opening times of the phone lines for death and bereavement benefits complaints, Disability Living Allowance (DLA) for people aged 65 or over complaints and State Pension and the Pension Service complaints have changed to 8am to 6pm Monday to Friday.
Updated the opening times of the phone line for Social Fund complaints to Monday to Friday, 8am to 5pm.
Updated Attendance Allowance opening hours.
Changed the Debt Management phone line opening times to Monday to Friday 8am to 7.30pm.

Updated the opening hours to 8am to 5pm for the phone lines for Attendance Allowance, Death and bereavement benefits, Disability Living Allowance (DLA) for people aged 65 or over, State Pension and the Pension Service.
Updated the opening times of the Carer's Allowance phone line to Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm.
Updated to give the current situation with complaints about State Pension age changes.
Updated the opening hours of the Employment and Support Allowance, Incapacity Benefit, Income Support and Jobseeker's Allowance phone lines to 8am to 5pm, Monday to Friday.
Added a link to the Video Relay Service (VRS) to the Social Fund section.
Added a link to the Video Relay Service (VRS) in the Pension Service section.
Added Video Relay Service (VRS) link to the Carers Allowance section.
Added Video Relay Service (VRS) details for Incapacity Benefit.
Added a link to the Video Relay Service(VRS)  in the death and bereavement benefits section.
Added a link to the Video Relay Service (VRS) in the Maternity Allowance section.
Updated the complaints procedure. Complaints are now dealt with by a Complaints team, rather than by a Complaints Resolution Manager.
Added a Video Relay Service (VRS) link to the Debt Management section.
Added a link to the Video Relay Service (VRS) to the Income Support section.
Added Video Relay Service (VRS) details to the Jobseeker's Allowance section.
Added links to the Video Relay Service (VRS).

If you want to complain about Jobcentre Plus, you should now write to your local Jobcentre Plus office instead of phoning. The opening times have some phone lines have changed to 9.30am to 3.30pm to help deal with the coronavirus (COVID-19) outbreak.
Changed opening times of the phone line for Disability Living Allowance for people aged 65 or over from Monday to Friday, 8am to 7.30pm to Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm.
Published updated information on complaints about State Pension age changes.
Added a definition of how DWP defines a complaint, details of what we cannot investigate as a complaint, a link to the Debt Management customer service standards and a link to DWP complaints data.
Updated the opening hours of the Jobcentre Plus telephone line for Income Support and Jobseeker's Allowance.
Updated the opening hours of the Jobcentre Plus telephone line for Income Support and Jobseeker's Allowance.
Added information about how DWP is dealing with complaints about State Pension age changes while the issue is before the High Court.
Updated the contact centre opening hours for Access to Work and Disability Living Allowance to 8:00am to 7:30pm.
Added Next Generation Text relay service phone numbers for people who cannot hear or speak on the phone.
Changed the opening times for the Carer's Allowance Unit to Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm.
Added translation
Added contact details for Universal Credit claimants that have an online account.
Updated the Welsh translation.
Added who to contact for 'new style' Employment and Support Allowance and 'new style' Jobseeker's Allowance complaints.
Added a link to the Department for Communities for people living in Northern Ireland.
Changed the postal address for DWP complaints to: DWP Complaints, Post Handling Site B, Wolverhampton, WV99 2GY
Updated guidance with new 0800 freephone numbers for Universal Credit.
Updated the section 'Complaints, mandatory reconsiderations and appeals are different' to link to new guidance.
Added details about how to complain about Disability Living Allowance for people aged 65 or over.
Added contact details to complain about Access to Work.
Updated link for Jobseeker's Allowance and Universal Credit complaint service.
Added link to service for making online complaints about Universal Credit.
First published.
